member.summary: Builds a composed of agents that operate concurrently on the same input, aggregating their outputs into a single collection.
member.param: The set of agents to compose into a concurrent workflow.

The aggregation function that accepts a list of the output messages from each and produces a single result list. If , the default behavior is to return a list containing the last message from each agent that produced at least one message.
member.returns: The built workflow composed of the supplied concurrent .

member.summary: Builds a composed of agents that operate concurrently on the same input, aggregating their outputs into a single collection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.AgentWorkflowBuilder.CreateHandoffBuilderWith(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent)
member.summary: Creates a new using as the starting agent in the workflow.
member.param: The agent that will receive inputs provided to the workflow.
member.returns: The builder for creating a workflow based on handoffs.
member.remarks: Handoffs between agents are achieved by the current agent invoking an provided to an agent via 's .. The must be capable of understanding those provided. If the agent ignores the tools or is otherwise unable to advertize them to the underlying provider, handoffs will not occur.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.AgentWorkflowBuilder.CreateGroupChatBuilderWith(System.Func{System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent},Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.GroupChatManager})

member.param: Function that will create the for the workflow instance. The manager will be provided with the set of agents that will participate in the group chat.
member.returns: The builder for creating a workflow based on handoffs.
member.remarks: Handoffs between agents are achieved by the current agent invoking an provided to an agent via 's .. The must be capable of understanding those provided. If the agent ignores the tools or is otherwise unable to advertize them to the underlying provider, handoffs will not occur.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.AggregatingExecutor`2
member.summary: Executes a workflow step that incrementally aggregates input messages using a user-provided aggregation function.
member.remarks: The aggregate state is persisted and restored automatically during workflow checkpointing. This executor is suitable for scenarios where stateful, incremental aggregation of messages is required, such as running totals or event accumulation.
member.typeparam: The type of input messages to be processed and aggregated.
The type representing the aggregate state produced by the aggregator function.
member.param: The unique identifier for this executor instance.

A function that computes the new aggregate state from the previous aggregate and the current input message. The function receives the current aggregate (or null if this is the first message) and the input message, and returns the updated aggregate.
Optional configuration settings for the executor. If null, default options are used.
Declare that this executor may be used simultaneously by multiple runs safely.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.AggregatingExecutor`2.#ctor(System.String,System.Func{`1,`0,`1},Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.ExecutorOptions,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Executes a workflow step that incrementally aggregates input messages using a user-provided aggregation function.
member.remarks: The aggregate state is persisted and restored automatically during workflow checkpointing. This executor is suitable for scenarios where stateful, incremental aggregation of messages is required, such as running totals or event accumulation.
member.typeparam: The type of input messages to be processed and aggregated.
The type representing the aggregate state produced by the aggregator function.
member.param: The unique identifier for this executor instance.

A function that computes the new aggregate state from the previous aggregate and the current input message. The function receives the current aggregate (or null if this is the first message) and the input message, and returns the updated aggregate.
Optional configuration settings for the executor. If null, default options are used.
Declare that this executor may be used simultaneously by multiple runs safely.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.MessageHandlerAttribute
member.summary: Marks a method as a message handler for source-generated route configuration. The method signature determines the input type and optional output type.

remarks.para:
Methods marked with this attribute must have a signature matching one of the following patterns:
void Handler(TMessage, IWorkflowContext)
void Handler(TMessage, IWorkflowContext, CancellationToken)
ValueTask Handler(TMessage, IWorkflowContext)
ValueTask Handler(TMessage, IWorkflowContext, CancellationToken)
TResult Handler(TMessage, IWorkflowContext)
TResult Handler(TMessage, IWorkflowContext, CancellationToken)
ValueTask<TResult> Handler(TMessage, IWorkflowContext)
ValueTask<TResult> Handler(TMessage, IWorkflowContext, CancellationToken)
The containing class must be partial and derive from .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.MessageHandlerAttribute.Yield
member.summary: Gets or sets the types that this handler may yield as workflow outputs.
member.remarks: If not specified, the return type (if any) is used as the default yield type. Use this property to explicitly declare additional output types or to override the default inference from the return type.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.MessageHandlerAttribute.Send
member.summary: Gets or sets the types that this handler may send as messages to other executors.
member.remarks: Use this property to declare the message types that this handler may send via during its execution. This information is used for protocol validation and documentation.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.SendsMessageAttribute
member.summary: Declares that an executor may send messages of the specified type.

remarks.para: Apply this attribute to an class to declare the types of messages it may send via . This information is used for protocol validation and documentation.
This attribute can be applied multiple times to declare multiple message types. It is inherited by derived classes, allowing base executors to declare common message types.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.SendsMessageAttribute.Type
member.summary: Gets the type of message that the executor may send.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.SendsMessageAttribute.#ctor(System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The type of message that the executor may send.
member.exception: is .

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.YieldsMessageAttribute
member.summary: Declares that an executor may yield messages of the specified type as workflow outputs.

remarks.para: Apply this attribute to an class to declare the types of messages it may yield via . This information is used for protocol validation and documentation.
This attribute can be applied multiple times to declare multiple output types. It is inherited by derived classes, allowing base executors to declare common output types.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.YieldsOutputAttribute
member.summary: Declares that an executor may yield messages of the specified type as workflow outputs.

remarks.para: Apply this attribute to an class to declare the types of messages it may yield via . This information is used for protocol validation and documentation.
This attribute can be applied multiple times to declare multiple output types. It is inherited by derived classes, allowing base executors to declare common output types.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.ChatForwardingExecutor
member.summary: A ChatProtocol executor that forwards all messages it receives. Useful for splitting inputs into parallel processing paths.
member.remarks: This executor is designed to be cross-run shareable and can be reset to its initial state. It handles multiple chat-related types, enabling flexible message forwarding scenarios. Thread safety and reusability are ensured by its design.
member.param: The unique identifier for the executor instance. Used to distinguish this executor within the system.
Optional configuration settings for the executor. If null, default options are used.

member.summary: Processes the current set of turn messages using the specified asynchronous processing function.
member.remarks: If the provided list of chat messages is null, an initial empty list is supplied to the processing function. If the processing function returns null, an empty list is used as the result.
member.param: A delegate that asynchronously processes a list of chat messages within the given workflow context and cancellation token, returning the processed list of chat messages or null.
The workflow context in which the messages are processed.
A token that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A ValueTask that represents the asynchronous operation. The result contains the processed list of chat messages, or an empty list if the processing function returns null.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.Checkpointing.FileSystemJsonCheckpointStore
member.summary: Provides a file system-based implementation of a JSON checkpoint store that persists checkpoint data and index information to disk using JSON files.
member.remarks: This class manages checkpoint storage by writing JSON files to a specified directory and maintaining an index file for efficient retrieval. It is intended for scenarios where durable, process-exclusive checkpoint persistence is required. Instances of this class are not thread-safe and should not be shared across multiple threads without external synchronization. The class implements IDisposable; callers should ensure Dispose is called to release file handles and system resources when the store is no longer needed.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.Checkpointing.PortableValueConverter
member.summary:
Provides special handling for serialization and deserialization, enabling delayed deserialization of the inner value. This is used to enable serialization/deserialization of objects whose type information is not available at the time of initial deserialization, e.g. user-defined state types.
This operates in conjuction with and to abstract away the speicfics of a given serialization format in favor of and and related methods.

member.remarks:
Pattern:
^(?<executorId>(((\\|\\|)|([^\\|]))*))\\|(?<scopeName>(@(((\\|\\|)|([^\\|]))*))?)\\|(?<key>(((\\|\\|)|([^\\|]))*)?)$
Options:
RegexOptions.ExplicitCapture | RegexOptions.CultureInvariant
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ "executorId" capture group.
○ Loop greedily any number of times.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match the string "||".
○ Match any character other than '|'.
○ Match '|'.
○ "scopeName" capture group.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ Match '@'.
○ Loop greedily any number of times.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match the string "||".
○ Match any character other than '|'.
○ Match '|'.
○ "key" capture group.
○ Loop greedily any number of times.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match the string "||".
○ Match any character other than '|'.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.Execution.UpdateKey
member.summary: Represents a unique key used to identify an update within a specific scope.
member.remarks: An is composed of a and a key, similar to . The difference is in how equality is determined: Unlike ScopeKey, two UpdateKeys that differ only by their ScopeId's ExecutorId are considered different, because updates coming from different executors need to be tracked separately, until they are marged (if appropriate) and published during a step transition.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.ExecutionMode.OffThread
member.summary: Normal streaming mode using the new channel-based implementation. Events stream out immediately as they are created.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.ExecutionMode.Lockstep
member.summary: Lockstep mode where events are batched per superstep. Events are accumulated and emitted after each superstep completes.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.ExecutionMode.Subworkflow
member.summary: A special execution mode for subworkflows - it functions like OffThread, but without the internal task running super steps, as they are implemented by being driven directly by the hosting workflow

Additional message types yielded as output by the handler. Defaults to empty.
Declare that this executor may be used simultaneously by multiple runs safely.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.InProc.InProcessRunner

remarks.para: enables step-by-step execution of a workflow graph entirely within the current process, without distributed coordination. It is primarily intended for testing, debugging, or scenarios where workflow execution does not require executor distribution.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.InProcessExecution.Lockstep

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.OpenTelemetryWorkflowBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for adding OpenTelemetry instrumentation to instances.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.OpenTelemetryWorkflowBuilderExtensions.WithOpenTelemetry(Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.WorkflowBuilder,System.Action{Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.Observability.WorkflowTelemetryOptions},System.Diagnostics.ActivitySource)
member.summary: Enables OpenTelemetry instrumentation for the workflow, providing comprehensive observability for workflow operations.
member.param: The to which OpenTelemetry support will be added.
An optional callback that provides additional configuration of the instance. This allows for fine-tuning telemetry behavior such as enabling sensitive data collection.

An optional to use for telemetry. If provided, this activity source will be used directly and the caller retains ownership (responsible for disposal). If , a shared default activity source named "Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows" will be used.
member.returns: The with OpenTelemetry instrumentation enabled, enabling method chaining.
member.exception: is .

remarks.para:
This extension adds comprehensive telemetry capabilities to workflows, including:
Distributed tracing of workflow execution
Executor invocation and processing spans
Edge routing and message delivery spans
Workflow build and validation spans
Error tracking and exception details
By default, workflow telemetry is disabled. Call this method to enable telemetry collection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.PortableValue.As``1
member.summary: Attempts to retrieve the underlying value as the specified type, deserializing if necessary.
member.remarks: If the underlying value implements delayed deserialization, this method will attempt to deserialize it to the specified type. If the value is already of the requested type, it is returned directly. Otherwise, the default value for TValue is returned. For value types, the default is not , UNLESS is nullable, e.g. int?.
member.typeparam: The type to which the value should be cast or deserialized.
member.returns: The value cast or deserialized to type TValue if possible; otherwise, the default value for type TValue.

member.summary: Provides a set of streaming aggregation functions for processing sequences of input values in a stateful, incremental manner.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.StreamingAggregators.First``2(System.Func{``0,``1})
member.summary: Creates a streaming aggregator that returns the result of applying the specified conversion function to the first input value.
member.remarks: Subsequent inputs after the first are ignored by the aggregator. This method is useful for scenarios where only the first occurrence in a stream is relevant. The conversion function is invoked at most once.
member.typeparam: The type of the input elements to be aggregated.
The type of the result produced by the conversion function.
member.param: A function that converts an input value of type to a result of type . This function is applied to the first input received.
member.returns: An aggregation function that yields the result of converting the first input using the specified function.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.StreamingAggregators.First``1
member.summary: Creates a streaming aggregator that returns the first input element.
member.typeparam: The type of the input elements to aggregate.
member.returns: A an aggrgation function that yields the first input element.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.StreamingAggregators.Last``2(System.Func{``0,``1})
member.summary: Creates a streaming aggregator that returns the result of applying the specified conversion to the most recent input value.
member.typeparam: The type of the input elements to be aggregated.
The type of the result produced by the conversion function.
member.param: A function that converts each input value to a result. Cannot be null.
member.returns: A aggregator function that yields the result of converting the last input received using the specified function.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.StreamingAggregators.Last``1
member.summary: Creates a streaming aggregator that returns the last element in a sequence.
member.typeparam: The type of elements in the input sequence.
member.returns: An aggregator function that yields the last element of the input.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.StreamingAggregators.Union``2(System.Func{``0,``1})
member.summary: Creates a streaming aggregator that produces the union of results by applying a conversion function to each input and accumulating the results.
member.typeparam: The type of the input elements to be aggregated.
The type of the result elements produced by the conversion function.
member.param: A function that converts each input element to a result element to be included in the union.
member.returns: An aggregator function that, for each input, returns an enumerable containing the result of converting every element produced so far.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.StreamingAggregators.Union``1
member.summary: Creates a streaming aggregator that produces the union of all input sequences of type TInput.
member.remarks: The resulting aggregator combines all input sequences into a single sequence containing distinct elements. The order of elements in the output sequence is not guaranteed.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the input sequences to be aggregated.
member.returns: An aggregator function, that, when applied to multiple input sequences, returns an containing the union of all elements from those sequences.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.StreamingRun.WatchStreamAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously streams workflow events as they occur during workflow execution.
member.remarks: This method yields instances in real time as the workflow progresses. The stream completes when a is encountered. Events are delivered in the order they are raised.
member.param: A that can be used to cancel the streaming operation. If cancellation is requested, the stream will end and no further events will be yielded, but this will not cancel the workflow execution.
member.returns: An asynchronous stream of objects representing significant workflow state changes. The stream ends when the workflow completes or when cancellation is requested.

member.summary: Processes all events from the workflow execution stream until completion.
member.remarks: This method continuously monitors the workflow execution stream provided by and invokes the for each event. If the callback returns a non- response, the response is sent back to the workflow using the handle.

member.returns: A that represents the asynchronous operation. The task completes when the workflow execution stream is fully processed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.WorkflowVisualizer.SanitizeMermaidNodeId(System.String)
member.summary: Converts a raw node ID into a Mermaid-safe identifier that preserves as much of the original text as possible. ASCII letters, digits, and underscores are kept as-is (including existing consecutive underscores). All other characters (including non-ASCII letters) are replaced with underscores, with consecutive invalid characters collapsed into a single underscore. A leading digit gets a prefix.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.WorkflowBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for configuring and building workflows using the WorkflowBuilder type.
member.remarks: These extension methods simplify the process of connecting executors, adding external calls, and constructing workflows with output aggregation. They are intended to streamline workflow graph construction and promote common patterns for chaining and aggregating workflow steps.

member.summary: Adds a sequential chain of executors to the workflow, connecting each executor in order so that each is executed after the previous one.
member.remarks: Each executor in the chain is connected so that execution flows from the source to each subsequent executor in the order provided.
member.param: The workflow builder to which the executor chain will be added.
The initial executor in the chain. Cannot be null.
An ordered sequence of executors to be added to the chain after the source.
member.returns: The original workflow builder instance with the specified executor chain added.
member.param: If set to , the same executor can be added to the chain multiple times.
member.exception: Thrown if there is a cycle in the chain.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.WorkflowBuilderExtensions.AddExternalCall``2(Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.WorkflowBuilder,Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.ExecutorBinding,System.String)
member.summary: Adds an external call to the workflow by connecting the specified source to a new input port with the given request and response types.
member.remarks: This method creates a bidirectional connection between the source and the new input port, allowing the workflow to send requests and receive responses through the specified external call. The port is configured to handle messages of the specified request and response types.
member.typeparam: The type of the request message that the external call will accept.
The type of the response message that the external call will produce.
member.param: The workflow builder to which the external call will be added.
The source executor representing the external system or process to connect. Cannot be null.
The unique identifier for the input port that will handle the external call. Cannot be null.
member.returns: The original workflow builder instance with the external call added.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.WorkflowBuilderExtensions.AddSwitch(Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.WorkflowBuilder,Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.ExecutorBinding,System.Action{Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows.SwitchBuilder})
member.summary: Adds a switch step to the workflow, allowing conditional branching based on the specified source executor.
member.remarks: Use this method to introduce conditional logic into a workflow, enabling execution to follow different paths based on the outcome of the source executor. The switch configuration defines the available branches and their associated conditions.
member.param: The workflow builder to which the switch step will be added. Cannot be null.
The source executor that determines the branching condition for the switch. Cannot be null.
An action used to configure the switch builder, specifying the branches and their conditions. Cannot be null.
member.returns: The workflow builder instance with the configured switch step added.

Enables as the default number handling for number types.
